children.heading: Laws governing; conservation and protection of resources
children.label: a Laws governing; conservation and protection of resources
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the park in accordance with this subchapter and with the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the national park system, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes,” approved (; ). The Secretary shall protect, manage, and administer the park in such manner as to conserve and protect the scenery, the natural, geologic, historic, and archaeological resources of the park, including fish and wildlife and to provide for the public use and enjoyment of the same in such a manner as to perpetuate these qualities for future generations.August 26, 191639 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1–41See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Fishing
children.label: b Fishing
children.text: The Secretary shall permit fishing on lands and waters under his jurisdiction within the park in accordance with the applicable laws of the United States and the State of Nevada, except that he may designate zones where, and periods when, no fishing may be permitted for reasons of public safety. Except in emergencies, any regulations prescribing such restrictions relating to fishing, shall be put into effect only after consultation with the appropriate State agency having jurisdiction over fishing activities.2So in original. The comma probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Preparation of management plan; submission to Congress; amendment of plan
children.label: c Preparation of management plan; submission to Congress; amendment of plan
children.text: After notice and opportunity for public hearing, the Secretary shall prepare a management plan for the park. The Secretary shall submit such plan to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States House of Representatives and with the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the United States Senate within three years after . Such plan may be amended from time to time. The plan shall include, but not be limited to, provisions related to grazing within the park to the extent permitted under subsection (e) and provisions providing for the appropriate management of fish and wildlife and fishing within the park in accordance with subsection (b). Such provisions shall be adopted only after consultation with the appropriate State agency having jurisdiction over fish and wildlife.October 27, 1986

children.heading: Withdrawal of lands from mining and mineral leasing
children.label: d Withdrawal of lands from mining and mineral leasing
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, Federal lands and interests therein, within the park, are withdrawn from disposition under the public lands laws and from entry or appropriation under the mining laws of the United States, from the operation of the mineral leasing laws of the United States, and from operation of the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970, as amended [ et seq.].30 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Grazing
children.label: e Grazing
children.text: Subject to such limitations, conditions, or regulations as he may prescribe, the Secretary may permit grazing on lands within the park to the same extent as was permitted on such lands as of . Grazing within the park shall be administered by the National Park Service.July 1, 1985

children.heading: Exchange of park grazing allotment for grazing allotment outside park
children.label: f Exchange of park grazing allotment for grazing allotment outside park

children.heading: Exchanges
children.label: 1 Exchanges
children.text: At the request of the permittee, or at the initiative of the Secretary, negotiations may take place at any time with holders of valid existing grazing permits and grazing leases on land within the park, for an exchange of all or part of their grazing allotments for allotments outside the park. No such exchange shall take place if, in the opinion of the affected Federal land management agency, the exchange would result in overgrazing of Federal lands.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire by donation valid existing permits and grazing leases authorizing grazing on land in the park.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: B Termination
children.text: The Secretary shall terminate a grazing permit or grazing lease acquired under subparagraph (A) so as to end grazing previously authorized by the permit or lease.

children.heading: Water-related range improvements
children.label: g Water-related range improvements
children.text: Existing water-related range improvements inside the park may be maintained by the Secretary or the persons benefitting from them, subject to reasonable regulation by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reservation to United States of new express or implied water or water-related right not established; exception
children.label: h Reservation to United States of new express or implied water or water-related right not established; exception
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to establish a new express or implied reservation to the United States of any water or water-related right with respect to the land described in : , That the United States shall be entitled to only that express or implied reserved water right which may have been associated with the initial establishment and withdrawal of Humboldt National Forest and the Lehman Caves National Monument from the public domain with respect to the land described in . No provision of this subchapter shall be construed as authorizing the appropriation of water, except in accordance with the substantive and procedural law of the State of Nevada.section 410mm of this titlesection 410mm of this titleProvided

children.heading: Cooperative agreements with Federal and other agencies; interpretation of Great Basin physiographic region
children.label: i Cooperative agreements with Federal and other agencies; interpretation of Great Basin physiographic region
children.text: In order to encourage unified and cost-effective interpretation of the Great Basin physiographic region, the Secretary is authorized and encouraged to enter into cooperative agreements with other Federal, State, and local public departments and agencies providing for the interpretation of the Great Basin physiographic region. Such agreements shall include, but not be limited to, authority for the Secretary to develop and operate interpretive facilities and programs on lands and waters outside of the boundaries of such park, with the concurrence of the owner or administrator thereof.
